authority.registry_source: repo-governed tool_catalog.json and linked operator docs

environment_contract.notes: Operator tools should resolve DCOIR local configuration from Machine/System environment scope. Process-scope placeholders such as C:\path\to\dcoir-collector are invalid and should be rejected. Shared environment/path helpers live in modules/Dcoir.Common and git-lane self-contained helpers live in modules/Dcoir.Git. Tool-specific variables should use the documented canonical environment variable names; Airtable tools use DCOIR_AIRTABLE_TOKEN and DCOIR_AIRTABLE_BASE_ID.

module_ecosystem.role: paths, logging, JSON, validation, filesystem, safe names, timestamps, and shared context

self-contained git-lane helpers: Machine/System env lookup, placeholder rejection, UTF-8 logging, git executable discovery, native argument quoting, logged git process execution, branch checks, clean-tree checks, fetch, fast-forward pull, and ahead/behind analysis
module_ecosystem.validated_status: validated for git diagnostic, safe pre-pull, snapshot, and repo patch apply tools

module_ecosystem.role: gh auth, GitHub Actions API wrappers, run/job lookup
ChatGPT-friendly ZIP packaging wrapper and staging extension point
manifest parsing, dispatch, monitor, fail-fast gates, parallel execution throttle, capture, cleanup, summaries, exit codes

module_ecosystem.role: repo-relative path safety, safe names, path normalization, under-root checks, text-file filtering, binary sniffing, targeted staging, and UTF-8 logging
module_ecosystem.validated_status: validated by text-only and targeted snapshot smoke tests

module_ecosystem.role: repo patch path safety, payload-root resolution, allowed target roots, hashing, and UTF-8 logging

tools.tool_name: DCOIR ChatGPT Apply-In Single Payload Builder
tools.notes: Preferred builder/validator for chatgpt-apply-in staging. Builds one ZIP with root apply_manifest.json and files/, base64 encodes it as exactly payload.zip.b64, validates ZIP open, manifest, files/, safe request id, no truncation marker, no parts/chunks, and base64 round trip. This tool intentionally does not support payload.zip.b64.parts, part files, chunk manifests, or chunk markers. If connector limits are hit, reduce the batch size and rebuild a smaller single payload.zip.b64.
Validated. Imports Dcoir.Git for Machine/System env lookup, UTF-8 logging, native git invocation, branch/status, diff, stash, and graph diagnostics.

Validated. Imports Dcoir.Git for shared environment, UTF-8 logging, stash, fetch, fast-forward pull, safe new-stash detection, stash reapply, and conflict-safe stop behavior. Does not apply older existing stashes when no new stash is created.
Validated. Uses Dcoir.Git for git freshness/clean-tree checks and Dcoir.Snapshot for repo-relative path safety, targeted copy, and UTF-8 logging. Missing requested paths are hard failures.

Validated. Uses Dcoir.Git and Dcoir.Snapshot for read-only full-repo text snapshots, repo-relative paths, excluded directories/extensions, binary sniffing, generated manifest, and ChatGPT-friendly ZIP output.

Validated. Manifest-driven repo patch/apply helper using Dcoir.Git and Dcoir.RepoPatch. Supports payload root detection, wrapper-root stripping, explicit repo-relative copy maps, git rm deletion lists, allowed target roots, pre/post hash checks, PowerShell 5.1 compatibility, logs, and result JSON. WhatIfOnly and real fixture apply smokes passed.

Reusable ZIP helper for DCOIR diagnostics and upload bundles. Creates rootless ChatGPT-optimized ZIPs with forward-slash archive paths, priority triage entries first, diagnostic_index.md, captured_files.json, zip_manifest.json, hidden/system metadata filtering, optional UTF-8 normalization, and no wrapper-root junk.

Stable public entrypoint backed by reusable modules: Dcoir.Common, Dcoir.GitHub, Dcoir.Packaging, and Dcoir.Actions. Owns manifest parsing, dispatch guardrails, monitoring, fail-fast gates, parallel workflow execution throttle, evidence capture, artifact download, ChatGPT-friendly ZIP packaging, cleanup_output_folder_after_zip, summaries, and exit codes.
Harness-only wrapper. Imports Dcoir.Common for shared env/JSON/logging helpers, creates dry-run/watch/live guarded manifests, and executes the Actions Workflow Orchestrator.
Harness-only fail-fast same-workflow mode/suite ladder runner. Imports Dcoir.Common for shared env/JSON/safe-name helpers, creates a sequential ladder manifest, and executes the orchestrator.

Read-only Airtable schema, metadata, and record export tool. Requires DCOIR_AIRTABLE_TOKEN and DCOIR_AIRTABLE_BASE_ID in Machine/System environment scope. Uses Dcoir.Airtable and the ChatGPT-friendly ZIP helper. Captures base schema, table metadata, field metadata/options, view metadata, records, manifests, command context, transcripts, and metadata coverage notes. Keep environment token/base values non-disclosed. Automations, extensions/apps, interfaces, scripting extension code, and workspace admin inventory are explicitly marked unsupported unless a future Airtable API endpoint and token scope are added. First diagnostic can use -ExportMode SchemaOnly -ProbeUnsupportedMetadata; full DCOIR snapshot should use -ExportMode FullRecords -FullRecordDump -MetadataScope All -ProbeUnsupportedMetadata. Use -RedactLikelySecrets only when explicitly requested.

parameters.RedactLikelySecrets: optional switch for explicitly requested redacted samples or unknown/non-DCOIR Airtable bases; not the DCOIR full-export default
parameters.MaxRecordsPerTable: integer cap; 0 means unbounded in FullRecords mode
parameters.TableList: optional comma-separated table names or table IDs

tools.validated_status: validated by 2026-05-03 schema-only smoke, bounded live export, and full-record export artifacts. Latest unredacted full export produced 24 table schemas, 24 record files, and 1134 records. Operator policy update: DCOIR Airtable contents are scrubbed upstream; future DCOIR full exports should run unredacted unless operator requests a redacted sample.

tools.output_pattern: caller-owned target_records.json; planned_payload.json; execution_summary.json; after_readback_verification.json; error_report.json on failure
tools.notes: Reusable module for scripts that need Airtable record inserts through chatgpt-exec or operator-side PowerShell. Provides root JSON array parsing, field-id readback under Set-StrictMode, create-missing-by-key semantics, Airtable batch create with max batch size 10, duplicate/missing expected-key after-readback verification, and reusable planned_payload/execution/readback result structures. It does not update, delete, merge, or enqueue Delete Queue rows. Use when chatgpt-exec work discovers a reusable Airtable insert/readback pattern instead of leaving it as one-off staging code.
tools.validated_status: validated by dcoir-db-redesign-wbs-scaffold-run3-20260508-2025z artifact: 52 rows created, 72 plan records after readback, zero missing expected keys, zero duplicate expected keys, empty stderr, script-level success.

tools.output_pattern: ChatGPT apply-in payload staging folder/ZIP and payload report under DCOIR_DOWNLOADS_DIR
tools.notes: Active apply-in staging helper. Use for GitHub Actions apply-in payload preparation. Core skill parity runbook prefers bounded batches; if a four-skill batch fails, retry 2+2; one-skill payload only for natural final remainder or explicit operator-approved test/retry.

tools.output_pattern: caller-owned uploadable log path returned on success and failure
tools.notes: Module-level logging standard for operator tools. New/reworked local tools should prefer this module over ad hoc logging and should return an uploadable log path.

Active schema-backed WBS09 manifest audit gate. Use before broad view configuration or any manifest-driven Airtable UI pass; source/schema freshness belongs with dcoir-airtable-schema-cache and validation readiness with dcoir-validation-orchestrator.

tools.output_pattern: dry-run/apply issue close logs and summaries
tools.notes: Guarded bulk issue closure helper. Dry-run first; use only with explicit operator intent and token/config preconditions satisfied.

tools.output_pattern: chatgpt exec request/report artifacts and output ZIPs
tools.version_or_hash: validated exec lane; example restage commit 5fdfe97b6f4296de2048a6330a9f8fb76d2b4a5e
tools.notes: Active ChatGPT exec lane harness. Validated sequence: create apply-in payload that writes one exec request JSON, verify apply-in report and exec request readback, then restage the already-created request JSON without [skip ci] if needed to trigger exec.

tools.output_pattern: caller-owned planned_payload.json; execution_summary.json; after_readback_verification.json; error_report.json on failure
tools.notes: Reusable Airtable update helper module. Uses choice names for Airtable select readback/write comparison and keeps choice IDs as metadata only. Use when a branch discovers reusable update/readback behavior rather than one-off staging code.

tools.tool_name: DCOIR Work Items Schema Cleanup Tool
tools.output_pattern: cleanup dry-run/apply/verify logs and config-driven output artifacts
tools.notes: Table-cleanup support tool. Current Work Items cleanup no longer expects retired DELETE-prefixed fields or Queue Control active_work_items link. Future generic cleanup should use config files rather than table-specific environment variables.

tools.tool_name: DCOIR Airtable WBS09 UI Native View Creator/Configurator
tools.version_or_hash: last good configure source commit 9b423f84cae47c094a5c6f9af8f0bde8d37fba76; view-readback foundation commit 2beb3e7a6367c24185da284b34e1fa7d595fa9ae
tools.notes: Browser/Playwright WBS09 native view creation/configuration tool. Creation and some one-view configure paths have passed earlier bounded smoke tests, but repair/configure action primitives failed on checkbox filters, relative-date dropdowns, and second-sort insertion during 2026-05-10 recovery. Do not run broad rollout or represent repair automation as ready until redesigned around discover -> plan -> execute -> verify.

tools.tool_name: DCOIR Airtable WBS09 Native View Panel Readback
tools.notes: Read-only browser/UI evidence tool for saved Airtable native Filter/Sort panel state. Does not create or repair views. Use as verification foundation for future create/repair pipeline.

sweep_notes.purpose: Merged discoverability gaps found between local operator_tools snapshot and the GitHub catalog. Added current WBS09 readback/create-configure surfaces to the main catalog so fresh sessions do not need chat context.
known_limitations: Some historical addendum files still reflect legacy Airtable registry staging and should not be treated as current authority.
WBS09 create/configure automation is documented as draft_limited_do_not_use_for_broad_repair until redesigned around discover -> plan -> execute -> verify.
